headlines.title: Oil tanker traffic in Strait of Hormuz jumps after U.S. and Iran implement deal to open sea lane
headlines.summary: The U.S.-Iran deal has raised questions about how the Strait of Hormuz will be governed after the toll-free period ends.

headlines.title: Musk's SpaceX stake is worth over $1 trillion. Here are the other billionaire shareholders
headlines.summary: SpaceX shares were up 37% after its historic debut last week, which offered shares at a set price of $135.

headlines.title: Memory crisis hits such extremes that 'even Apple can't be safe'
headlines.summary: Apple appears poised to take the rare step of increasing prices to deal with what CEO Tim Cook called an "unsustainable" memory shortage.

headlines.title: DOJ rebuffs judge's request to put in writing it won't move forward with 'anti-weaponization' fund
headlines.summary: The DOJ in May announced it was creating the fund as part of a settlement of President Donald Trump's $10 billion lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service.

headlines.title: The riskiest SpaceX stock trade of all had a big first week
headlines.summary: SpaceX's IPO did not just mint the biggest debut in market history and a trillionaire, but it also triggered a historic land grab in risky leveraged ETFs.

headlines.title: Trump DNI pick Pulte poised to get access to U.S. intelligence despite congressional bid to thwart
headlines.summary: With President Donald Trump halting his nomination of  U.S. Attorney Jay Clayton as director of national intelligence, Bill Pulte is set to take on the role.

headlines.title: The average SpaceX buyer post-IPO is almost under water after two-day slide
headlines.summary: Shares of SpaceX fell as much as 7% Thursday to $178, leaving the stock trading roughly in line with its volume-weighted average price of just under $180.

headlines.title: Kalshi traders see greater than 50% odds the Fed will hike rates this year
headlines.summary: The Federal Reserve suggested higher rates could be in the cards, and traders on Kalshi see growing odds of a hike in 2026.

headlines.title: Why the oil may start flowing through the Strait of Hormuz faster than many believe
headlines.summary: Brian Sullivan's direct conversations with industry executives and experts based both here and in the Middle East indicate ships will start steaming.

headlines.title: CME CEO Terrence Duffy says the exchange operator will sue CFTC over perpetual futures
headlines.summary: Outgoing CME CEO Terrence Duffy said that the exchange operator will sue the CFTC over the agency’s decision to approve perpetual futures.

headlines.title: Jeffrey Gundlach says Fed's Warsh is not going to be the 'easy money' chairman many hoped for
headlines.summary: Gundlach said Warsh's stance reduces the risk of overly accommodative monetary policy that could reignite inflation and push longer-term borrowing costs higher.

headlines.title: 'Passive' investors who dodged bitcoin are now forced to own SpaceX, which is three times more volatile
headlines.summary: Many advisors and money-managers invested in index funds who will soon be owners of Elon Musk's astronomic ambitions

headlines.title: My two sons will inherit a $30,000 annuity from their grandmother. What should I do with the money?
headlines.summary: “My understanding is that they have five years to withdraw the money.”

headlines.title: Employers to college students: Never mind that 4.0 GPA. Go out and get a summer job.
headlines.summary: College students with any sort of work experience on their résumés are twice as likely to be employed shortly after graduating.

headlines.title: ‘We are habitually frugal’: My wife and I have money. How do we help our children without ruining their independence?
headlines.summary: “Partly due to some mental-health issues, they may continue to go through life living paycheck to paycheck, or worse.”

headlines.title: ‘I’ll probably be working until I die’: I’m 60, work as a waiter and have $2,000 in a Roth IRA. What will happen to me?
headlines.summary: “I’m afraid I’m in a bad place.”

headlines.title: Warsh’s new task forces give the Fed wiggle room to put off changing rates until December
headlines.summary: During his first press conference as Fed chair Wednesday, Kevin Warsh repeated one refrain in his answers to many reporters’ questions: A task force is looking into it.

headlines.title: Trump picked Kevin Warsh to cut rates. The new Fed chief just told us he has other plans.
headlines.summary: Here’s what the central bank’s hawkish agenda means for your money.
